title: Engineering Onboarding Checklist
summary: What to expect in your first 2 weeks: access, learning, shipping your first PR.
body: Week 1: access & local setup, read top 5 ADRs, shadow standups. Week 2: trace one feature end-to-end, ship a small PR, attend an incident review. Buddy will check in twice a week.
author: People Ops

title: ADR-027: Multi-tenant data isolation
summary: Why we picked row-level security over per-tenant schemas.
body: Row-level security in Postgres gives us tenant isolation without an explosion of schemas. We accept the performance trade-off in exchange for operational simplicity.
author: Priya Mehta

title: Secrets & Credentials
summary: How to request, rotate, and revoke credentials safely.
body: All secrets live in Vault. Request access via the self-serve portal. Rotations happen automatically every 30 days. Never commit secrets — pre-commit hooks block known patterns.
author: Security Team

title: Frontend Component Conventions
summary: Naming, composition, testing patterns we follow across web apps.
body: Use the design system primitives where possible. Components are presentational by default; data-fetching lives in hooks. Co-locate tests with components.
author: Web Platform

title: Incident Response Runbook
summary: What to do when an alert fires: triage, comms, resolution, postmortem.
body: Step 1: acknowledge in PagerDuty. Step 2: open incident channel. Step 3: page next escalation if no progress in 15m. Step 4: blameless postmortem within 5 business days.
author: SRE

title: API Design Guidelines
summary: REST conventions, error format, versioning, idempotency.
body: Use plural resource names. Return RFC-7807 problem+json for errors. Version in the URL (/v1/...). Mutating endpoints must accept Idempotency-Key.
author: Marco Bianchi

title: Local Development Tips
summary: Speed up your inner loop: profiles, scripts, IDE setup.
body: Use the included `make dev-fast` target to skip integration containers. Enable EditorConfig + Prettier on save. Pre-warm node_modules with `pnpm fetch`.
author: DevEx Team

title: Performance Budget
summary: Hard limits on bundle size, p95 latency, and DB query time.
body: Frontend bundle: <250 KB gzipped per route. API p95: <300ms. DB queries: <50ms on hot paths. CI fails builds that regress these.
author: Performance Guild
